success_surface: Cell-level manipulation with special data types (timedelta) requiring precise round-up logic while preserving number formats
effective_behaviors: Inspecting actual data types before processing to confirm value structure
Using openpyxl instead of pandas when special data types must be preserved
Processing only within the specified answer_position range
Converting timedelta objects properly using total_seconds() method
Applying ceiling function for upward rounding calculations
Explicitly setting target cell number formats per requirements
Handling null/empty source values gracefully without errors
Including verification logging after saving to confirm correctness
evidence: Agent ran diagnostic code to check B1/B2 values were datetime.timedelta objects before calculating
Solution used load_workbook() from openpyxl rather than pandas.read_excel() which would have lost timedelta type
Loop iterated range(1, 3) matching answer_position C1:C2 constraint exactly
Calculation used b_cell_value.total_seconds() / 3600 to extract hours from timedelta

memory_items.title: Data Type Preservation Strategy
memory_items.lesson: When manipulating special data types like timedeltas that require conversion logic, load workbooks with openpyxl instead of pandas to maintain native object types.
memory_items.generalization: Use openpyxl.load_workbook() for cell-level operations involving non-standard data types that may be converted to static values by pandas.read_excel().

memory_items.title: Pre-Processing Data Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Always verify actual data types and formats in source cells before applying transformations.
memory_items.generalization: Run diagnostic queries to inspect cell.value types and number_format attributes on target ranges prior to implementing business logic.

memory_items.title: Scope-Constrained Manipulation
memory_items.lesson: Restrict all modifications to the exact cell range specified in the instruction parameters.
memory_items.generalization: Honor answer_position constraints by limiting row/column loops to the minimum necessary range, avoiding unintended changes to other cells.

memory_items.title: Format Compliance Enforcement
memory_items.lesson: Explicitly set number_format properties on modified cells when specific formats are required.
memory_items.generalization: After writing calculated values, always apply the required number_format explicitly rather than relying on default Excel formatting.

memory_items.title: Null-Safe Iteration Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Include defensive checks for None/empty values when iterating over cell ranges.
memory_items.generalization: Wrap calculation logic in conditional blocks that handle missing source values gracefully, either skipping or assigning blank outputs.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Special Data Type Handling
- When source cells contain special Python objects (e.g., timedelta, datetime), prefer openpyxl over pandas to preserve native types during read/write operations.
- Validate data types before applying conversion formulas; use object methods (e.g., total_seconds()) appropriate to the detected type.

### Target Range Precision
- Strictly confine all write operations to the specified answer_position range to avoid collateral modifications.
- Iterate using explicit row/column limits matching the instruction's cell range specification.

### Format Requirements Compliance
- Always set number_format explicitly on newly written cells to match task specifications (e.g., 'General', 'Custom [h]:mm').
- Do not assume inherited formatting will satisfy requirements; override where necessary.

### Defensive Processing Patterns
- Include None/empty value checks before performing calculations on source cells.
- Assign None or blank outputs when source data is unavailable to maintain row alignment integrity.

### Verification Best Practices
- Log verification output showing updated cell values and formats after save operations.
- Use print statements to confirm both numerical results and format properties match expected outcomes.
